name: Bob
inventory: Adventurer's pack (backpack, bedroll, mess kit, tinderbox, 10 torches, 10 days rations, waterskin, 50ft hempen rope)
features: Dwarven Resilience (advantage on saves vs poison, resistance to poison damage)
Stonecunning (double proficiency on History checks related to stonework)
backstory:
Bob grew up in the iron-veined halls of Dun Morath, a dwarven stronghold carved deep beneath the Frostspire Mountains. The youngest of seven brothers, he was never the strongest or the cleverest, but he was the one who sat with the dying. When a plague of creeping rot swept the lower mines, it was Bob who tended the sick while his brothers sealed the tunnels. He prayed to Moradin the Forgefather — not for miracles, but for steady hands. The hands steadied, and the light came.

He left Dun Morath not out of ambition but obligation. The temple elders spoke of a darkness spreading in the surface lands, a corruption that smelled like the rot from the mines. Bob packed his mace, kissed the stone threshold, and climbed toward the sun for the first time in forty years.

He doesn't talk much about what he saw in the mines. He doesn't talk much at all. But when trouble comes, Bob is the one still standing, hands steady, light burning.
